name: Proton (Particle Physics)
description: JavaScript particle engine with full physics simulation — gravity, attraction, repulsion, springs, emitters and zones. GPU-accelerated via canvas/WebGL. Burst, fireworks, flowing particles and warp-trail effects.
head: <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/proton-engine@6.3.0/build/proton.min.js"></script>
body:
<script>
(function() {
  if (typeof Proton === 'undefined') return;
  var container = document.querySelector('.proton-canvas-container');
  if (!container) return;

  var canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
  canvas.width = container.offsetWidth;
  canvas.height = container.offsetHeight;
  canvas.style.cssText = 'position:absolute;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;pointer-events:none;';
  container.appendChild(canvas);

  var proton = new Proton();
  var emitter = new Proton.Emitter();

  // Emit 1 particle per frame, infinite
  emitter.rate = new Proton.Rate(new Proton.Span(1, 3), 0.05);

  // Particle appearance
  emitter.addInitialize(new Proton.Mass(1));
  emitter.addInitialize(new Proton.Radius(2, 6));
  emitter.addInitialize(new Proton.Life(1.5, 3));
  emitter.addInitialize(new Proton.Velocity(new Proton.Span(1, 3), new Proton.Span(0, 360), 'polar'));

  // Colour fade indigo→mint
  emitter.addBehaviour(new Proton.Color('#5b4dff', '#2dd4bf'));
  emitter.addBehaviour(new Proton.Alpha(1, 0));
  emitter.addBehaviour(new Proton.Scale(1, 0));
  emitter.addBehaviour(new Proton.Gravity(0.5));

  emitter.p.x = canvas.width / 2;
  emitter.p.y = canvas.height * 0.6;
  emitter.emit();

  // Follow mouse
  container.addEventListener('mousemove', function(e) {
    var rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
    emitter.p.x = e.clientX - rect.left;
    emitter.p.y = e.clientY - rect.top;
  });

  proton.addEmitter(emitter);
  var renderer = new Proton.CanvasRenderer(canvas);
  proton.addRenderer(renderer);

  (function tick() {
    requestAnimationFrame(tick);
    proton.update();
  })();
})();
</script>
